briefing.opening: Good morning. As the specialist spokesperson for climate change, I will now begin today's climate briefing on April 30, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis, Everyday Threats
sections.content: Today's news clearly demonstrates that climate change is no longer a distant future problem but a tangible threat to our lives. Domestically, islands are at risk of disappearing due to climate change and population decline, and 6 out of 10 citizens reported experiencing direct impacts of climate change [1, 5]. Internationally, a shocking report indicated that record-breaking heatwaves in Europe in 2025 led to a warming rate in the region that was twice the global average. Furthermore, the North Sea's sea surface temperature reached an all-time high, raising the possibility of attracting invasive species like great white sharks [15, 111]. Additionally, warnings from experts that climate change could add hundreds of dollars annually to homeowners' insurance premiums for residents of Con County, along with news of climate-altering gases being emitted from reservoirs, once again underscore the economic and environmental ramifications of the climate crisis [107, 100].

sections.title: 🌱 Carbon Neutrality Technology Innovation and Enhanced Public-Private Cooperation
sections.content: Amidst these threats, domestic and international efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society continue steadily. Notably, the Electronics and Telecommunications Research Institute (ETRI) has embarked on a 'Carbon-Neutral Smart Farm' demonstration, utilizing Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology to manage pig farming in the Jeju region and reduce carbon emissions in the livestock sector, thereby achieving both productivity enhancement and environmental protection [19, 20, 21, 28, 32, 36, 55]. Furthermore, the Korea Environmental Preservation Agency, the Ministry of Climate, and Danggeun Market have signed a business agreement to promote a culture of carbon neutrality practices, encouraging public participation by quantifying the environmental value of second-hand transactions [11, 12, 25, 27, 31, 47, 48, 52, 53]. The introduction of the Korean Green Taxonomy (K-Taxonomy) is also enhancing the transparency of companies' eco-friendly activities [22, 51]. In addition, active movements towards achieving carbon neutrality goals have been observed in various sectors, including Jeonju City's hosting of the Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth Committee [33, 38, 43], Gwangju City's greenhouse gas reduction achievements through 'Corporate Carbon Action' [58], and the collaboration between Schneider Electric Korea and Konkuk University to foster carbon-neutral talent [30, 34, 39, 44, 45].

sections.title: ⚡ Renewable Energy Expansion, Towards a Sustainable Future
sections.content: Positive news continues to emerge in the renewable energy sector, which is central to energy transition. The International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) emphasized the urgent need for a renewable energy-centric energy structure transition amidst energy security threats posed by Middle East risks [59]. Indeed, China and India are projected to see their renewable energy generation surpass coal power generation in 2025, alongside reductions in fossil fuel power [62]. Domestically, Korea Midland Power (KOMIPO) has fully launched its Renewable Energy Operations Headquarters, introducing an AI-based smart operating system [60, 65, 80]. Public institutions in Gyeonggi Province have also achieved their 'RE101' goal of transitioning 100% of their electricity consumption to renewable energy ahead of schedule [85]. Hyundai Engineering's successful project financing for a North American solar power plant [69, 82], Gumi National Industrial Complex's efforts to expand solar power generation facilities [70, 77, 84], and the news of domestic solar-related companies collectively achieving profitability in the first quarter [78, 87, 88] all demonstrate the revitalization and growth potential of the renewable energy market. However, the substantial debt of Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) and the necessity of grid and site planning for renewable energy expansion remain key challenges that need to be addressed [7, 61, 63].

sections.title: 📰 Policy, Research, and Awareness Enhancement for Climate Change Response
sections.content: Policy discussions, research, and efforts to raise public awareness for climate change response are also active. Domestically, the government announced its bid to host COP33 in the South Coast-Namjung region in 2028 [6], and efforts to monitor climate change-causing substances continue at frontline stations like the Jeju Gosan Global Atmosphere Watch Observatory [2, 9]. The Korea Legislation Research Institute evaluated policy achievements and explored areas for improvement through the '5 Years of Carbon Neutrality, Local Government Achievements and Limitations' forum [17, 56], and Seoul National University hosted an invited lecture on the threats and opportunities in the era of carbon neutrality [26]. Internationally, the Climate Change Education Act has been reintroduced in Congress [97], and the Kamin Science Center has opened a climate change exhibition, focusing on education and awareness dissemination [103]. Interestingly, research indicating that Artificial Intelligence (AI) computer models are not as proficient as traditional models in extreme weather prediction [112] suggests that alongside technological advancements, the importance of existing research should also be considered. Meanwhile, AESC's achievement of an A- rating in the 2025 CDP Climate Change assessment, the highest evaluation among global battery manufacturers, demonstrates international recognition of corporate eco-friendly efforts [109, 110].

briefing.closing: As we have confirmed in today's briefing, climate change is having a severe impact across all sectors of our society. However, at the same time, hopeful steps towards a sustainable future are being taken through technological innovation, policy efforts, and public-private cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing on April 30, 2026.
